LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT BARNECUT ROOFING COMPANY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT REQUIRES ALLEGATIONS OF ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE KNOWLEDGE OF THE PERIL TO BE APPREHENDED; ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE KNOWLEDGE THAT INJURY IS A PROBABLE, AS OPPOSED TO A POSSIBLE, RESULT OF THE DANGER; AND CONSCIOUS FAILURE TO ACT TO AVOID THE PERIL. (BAIN V. WESTERN PACIFIC R. CO. (1976) 56 CAL.APP.3D 902, 905.) THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (SAC) ALLEGES MANY SPECIFIC ACTIONS TAKEN BY DEFENDANTS THAT ARE CHARACTERIZED AS IMPROPER METHODS AND THAT DEFENDANTS "KNEW, OR AS LICENSED CONTRACTORS WHO SPECIALIZED IN WATERPROOFING, SHOULD HAVE KNOWN" THAT USING THESE IMPROPER METHODS CREATED THE RISK OF LEAKAGE. (SAC 50.) THE SAC ALSO ALLEGES THAT AS SPECIALIZED CONTRACTORS, DEFENDANTS HAD KNOWLEDGE THAT EACH OF THE IMPROPER INSTALLATION METHODS, ESPECIALLY WHEN OCCURRING TOGETHER, WOULD MAKE THE LIKELIHOOD OF LEAKAGE PROBABLE. (SAC 51.) FINALLY, PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT BY TAKING THESE ACTIONS WITH THE KNOWLEDGE OF THE RISK CREATED AND THE PROBABILITY OF ITS OCCURRENCE, DEFENDANTS CONSCIOUSLY FAILED TO AVOID THE HARM. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFFS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE THE REQUISITE ELEMENTS OF A WILLFUL MISCONDUCT CAUSE OF ACTION. MOREOVER, THE PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT DEGLIANTONI WAS AN OFFICER OR DIRECTOR OF DEFENDANT BARNECUT, AND THAT HE WAS PERSONALLY GUILTY OF THE WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, THEREBY SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGING LIABILITY ON THE PART OF BOTH DEFENDANT DEGLIANTONI AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND DEFENDANT BARNECUT FOR THE WILLFUL MISCONDUCT OF ITS CORPORATE OFFICER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT GARY DEGLIANTONI'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT REQUIRES ALLEGATIONS OF ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE KNOWLEDGE OF THE PERIL TO BE APPREHENDED; ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE KNOWLEDGE THAT INJURY IS A PROBABLE, AS OPPOSED TO A POSSIBLE, RESULT OF THE DANGER; AND CONSCIOUS FAILURE TO ACT TO AVOID THE PERIL. (BAIN V. WESTERN PACIFIC R. CO. (1976) 56 CAL.APP.3D 902, 905.) THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (SAC) ALLEGES MANY SPECIFIC ACTIONS TAKEN BY DEFENDANTS THAT ARE CHARACTERIZED AS IMPROPER METHODS AND THAT DEFENDANTS "KNEW, OR AS LICENSED CONTRACTORS WHO SPECIALIZED IN WATERPROOFING, SHOULD HAVE KNOWN" THAT USING THESE IMPROPER METHODS CREATED THE RISK OF LEAKAGE. (SAC 50.) THE SAC ALSO ALLEGES THAT AS SPECIALIZED CONTRACTORS, DEFENDANTS HAD KNOWLEDGE THAT EACH OF THE IMPROPER INSTALLATION METHODS, ESPECIALLY WHEN OCCURRING TOGETHER, WOULD MAKE THE LIKELIHOOD OF LEAKAGE PROBABLE. (SAC 51.) FINALLY, PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT BY TAKING THESE ACTIONS WITH THE KNOWLEDGE OF THE RISK CREATED AND THE PROBABILITY OF ITS OCCURRENCE, DEFENDANTS CONSCIOUSLY FAILED TO AVOID THE HARM. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFFS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE THE REQUISITE ELEMENTS OF A WILLFUL MISCONDUCT CAUSE OF ACTION. MOREOVER, THE PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT DEGLIANTONI WAS AN OFFICER OR DIRECTOR OF DEFENDANT BARNECUT, AND THAT HE WAS PERSONALLY GUILTY OF THE WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, THEREBY SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGING LIABILITY ON THE PART OF BOTH DEFENDANT DEGLIANTONI AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND DEFENDANT BARNECUT FOR THE WILLFUL MISCONDUCT OF ITS CORPORATE OFFICER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS BARNECUT ROOFING, INC. AND GARY DEGLIANTONI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND GRANTED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFFS HAVE PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF AN AGREEMENT THAT DEFENDANTS WOULD INSTALL TWO PLIES OF BITUTHENE TO PREVENT LEAKS (SEE DECLARATION OF JAMES JOYCE 95: 17-25.) A TRIER OF FACT COULD FIND THAT AN AGREEMENT EXISTED BETWEEN THE PARTIES TO INSTALL TWO PLIES OF BITUTHENE AND DEFENDANTS' NONPERFORMANCE CONSTITUTED A BREACH. THEREFORE, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT PRECLUDING SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT/CONSCIOUS DISREGARD. DEFENDANTS HAVE MET THEIR BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING A TRIABLE ISSUE REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489 (302/HK)

Defendants Barnecut Roofing, Inc. And Gary Degliantoni'S Motion For Summary Adjudication (Control Date)
Off Calendar Jun-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Scott A. Williams, Williams & Gumbiner LLP, Tel. 415.755.1880, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Robert L. Davis, Law Offices of Ryan & Lifter, APC, Tel. 925.884.2080, for defendants Gary Degliantoni;
Phillip Bazzano, of Sellar Hazard & Lucia, Tel. 925.938.1430, for intervenor, Lexington Insurance Company, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Barnecut Roofing, Inc. And Gary Degliantoni's Motion For Summary Adjudication is Denied as to the sixth cause of action and granted as to the request for punitive damages. Plaintiffs have presented evidence of an agreement that Defendants would install two plies of Bituthane to prevent leaks (See Declaration of James Joyce 95: 17-25.) A trier of fact could find that an agreement existed between the parties to install two plies of Biththane and Defendants' nonperformance constituted a breach. Therefore, there is a triable issue of material fact precluding summary adjudication of the sixth cause of action for willful misconduct/conscious disregard. Defendants have met their burden of production and plaintiffs have not presented any evidence establishing a triable issue regarding punitive damages. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/HK)

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JEREMY KOTAS, ANTHONY PANTALEONI AND KOTAS/PANTALEONI ARCHITECTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
is Granted. Defendants have met their burden of establishing that the 10 year statute of limitations per CCP sec. 1375 expired on March 15, 2012, and Defendants were not added by a Doe amendment until April 9, 2013. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 24-25, 28.) Defendants have also met their initial burden of showing that the Doe amendment does not relate back to the filing of the original complaint because Plaintiffs cannot establish that they were genuinely ignorant of facts giving rise to Defendants' liability at the time the complaint was filed. (UMF 3-4, 8, 14-15, 17-20.) Plaintiffs have not raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether they were genuinely ignorant. Plaintiffs self-serving declarations that they were unaware of facts giving rise to Defendants' liability may be disregarded, as they are directly contradicted by the complaint and evidence. In the original complaint, Plaintiffs allege that "Defendants so negligently designed'as to directly cause, create and/or contribute to the Defects and resulting damages and losses." (Complaint, pars. 19, 24.) It is undisputed Plaintiffs were aware that Defendants were the architects for the project at the time the complaint was filed. (UMF 3-4, 8.) Further it is undisputed that, prior to the complaint being filed, the attorney for Plaintiff 2189 Market Street Owners Association sent Defendants a demand letter threatening a lawsuit, as well as a letter seeking a tolling stipulation that also threatened a lawsuit. (UMF 14-15, 17-20.) Plaintiffs have "failed to demonstrate that at the time the complaint was filed [they were] ignorant of facts having a bearing upon the liability of [Defendants]." (Snoke v. Bolen (1991) 235 Cal.App.3d 1427, 1432.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANT GARY DEGLIANTONI's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. A cause of action for willful misconduct requires allegations of actual or constructive knowledge of the peril to be apprehended; actual or constructive knowledge that injury is a probable, as opposed to a possible, result of the danger; and conscious failure to act to avoid the peril. (Bain v. Western Pacific R. Co. (1976) 56 Cal.App.3d 902, 905.) The Second Amended Complaint (SAC) alleges many specific actions taken by Defendants that are characterized as improper methods and that Defendants "knew, or as licensed contractors who specialized in waterproofing, should have known" that using these improper methods created the risk of leakage. (SAC 50.) The SAC also alleges that as specialized contractors, Defendants had knowledge that each of the improper installation methods, especially when occurring together, would make the likelihood of leakage probable. (SAC 51.) Finally, Plaintiffs allege that by taking these actions with the knowledge of the risk created and the probability of its occurrence, Defendants consciously failed to avoid the harm. Therefore, Plaintiffs sufficiently allege the requisite elements of a willful misconduct cause of action. Moreover, the Plaintiffs allege that Defendant Degliantoni was an officer or director of Defendant Barnecut, and that he was personally guilty of the willful misconduct, thereby sufficiently alleging liability on the part of both Defendant Degliantoni as an individual and Defendant Barnecut for the willful misconduct of its corporate officer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
